mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending corporate earnings weakness, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >65% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and credit availability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail sales and housing starts fail to predict positive quarterly returns with a t-statistic above 1.5 over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.02 during periods of rising interest rates, or positive returns occur in less than 45% of months with rising industrial production.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 200 indicate entrenched inflationary pressures that force tighter financial conditions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 200.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that supports corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to generate positive quarterly returns in >55% of occurrences, or the interaction term is statistically insignificant with a p-value above 0.10 in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product reflects stronger aggregate economic activity and corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth shows no statistical correlation with subsequent 20-day equity returns with Rank IC below 0.01 over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a contraction in manufacturing output and weakening supply-side momentum, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 5-day returns in >65% of instances following two consecutive months of industrial production declines.
